customer_anxiety_profile: Low expressed anxiety — cooperative, task-oriented, accepted in-person booking without pushback. However, the customer's initial preference was phone/Zoom, and the in-person booking was compliance-based rather than conviction-based. No emotional investment in the appointment was established.
unresolved_concerns: Customer's stated preference for phone or Zoom was overridden — closer should be prepared for a potential reschedule request or no-show
Son is on the title and was not included in the appointment — closer must confirm son's availability and role before or at the start of the visit
Roof type and age were never captured — closer should assess on arrival
sensitive_topics_raised: Townhome ownership structure (customer confirmed individual meter and roof section ownership, but roof age unknown)
exact_promises_rep_made: In-person appointment at customer's home at 7:30 the following evening
closer_should_know: The son is on the title and was not scheduled into this appointment — confirm his availability before the visit. If the son is an active decision-maker, the closer may be walking into a one-legged sit. Additionally, the customer initially preferred phone/Zoom and accepted in-person only after Carter's push; open with a brief value statement for why the in-person format serves them to convert compliance into buy-in.
